title: circt-reduce,firtool,llhd-sim: cleanup --help output, put options in category
body:
# Summary

Slightly improve `--help` output for a few tools by grouping the options for each tool explicitly and hiding unrelated options.

In the default build configuration, the output change is not significantly different for `circt-reduce` and `firtool` (I'll post the output below).  `llhd-sim` does have a number of low-level flags hidden, which I believe are not useful but this should be vetted.

Tests are extended, and in the case of `llhd-sim`, added, to check for this new output.

## Motivation

When building CIRCT against LLVM built as shared libraries (or library), as preferred by packagers of LLVM in e.g., most Linux distributions, this change greatly improves the usability of these tools (200 -> 38 options, or so, for circt-reduce, IIRC).

That was the original motivation, as I wanted to see what options these tools offered and finding them among the hundreds of CodeGen flags is not great.

## Why these tools?

The others would ideally be improved too, but they are mostly defined within MLIR so that's left as a separate effort.
This PR focuses on the tools we control the main + `cl::ParseCommandLine` call for.

# Downsides / Caveats

No easy way to discover options that are hidden, if they are needed.
This really only makes sense under assumption that they're not needed at all.  While they still would be parsed, discoverability is important if they're useful.

When reviewing the differences in `--help`, one option we may wish to preserve is the `--color` flag (which is in its own "Color Options" category).  I do not believe we have anything producing color output but if so we can include that in the list of command line "categories" we whitelist (it is overloaded to take an `ArrayRef` if needed).

MLIR options are still under `General options`.  This seems fine to me, as those options are not specific to each tool, but wanted to highlight it.  If preferred, we can from our tools walk the options and move mlir options to a different category, using `getRegisteredOptions` [using code like this example in the documentation](https://llvm.org/docs/CommandLine.html#the-cl-getregisteredoptions-function), but this seems messy and brittle re:assumptions like "assume MLIR options start with --mlir".
